LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF WAS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE WHAT PORTION OF THE CONTRACT WAS BREACHED AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO DO SO. AS TO THE THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION WAS FORECLOSED BY GOMES V. COUNTRYWIDE HOME LOANS, INC. (2011) 192 CAL. APP. 4TH 1149, 1152 N. 7, CERT. DENIED 132 S.CT. 419 (2011). AS TO THE EIGHTH, NINTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD ULTIMATE FACTS SUPPORTING THE CAUSES OF ACTION. AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED 15 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. PLAINTIFF SHELLEY WIENKE'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE OF COURT TO FILE A FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 473 IS GRANTED IN PART. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO FILE A FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT EXCEPT THAT PLAINTIFF IS NOT GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD THE PROPOSED ELEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE HOMEOWNER'S BILL OF RIGHTS. BEFORE FILING THE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF SHOULD MAKE ANY NECESSARY MODIFICATIONS SO THAT IT CONFORMS TO THE COURT'S JUNE 21, 2013 RULING SUSTAINING IN PART DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. TO BE HEARD IN DEPT. 302 AT 9:30 A.M. JUN-25-2012. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST THROUGH FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION. REGARDING THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD THAT DEFENDANT UNEQUIVOCALLY FAILED TO CONTACT HER PRIOR TO RECORDING THE NOTICE OF DEFAULT. REGARDING THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD THE LEGAL EFFECT OF THE CONTRACT AND WHAT PORTION OF THE CONTRACT DEFENDANT BREACHED, AND THAT PLAINTIFF PERFORMED UNDER THE CONTRACT OR WAS EXCUSED FROM PERFORMING. REGARDING THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD A BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT ARISING OUT OF A CONTRACT. REGARDING THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING DETRIMENTAL RELIANCE. REGARDING THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FRAUD WITH PARTICULARITY, INCLUDING THE "HOW, WHEN, WHERE, TO WHOM, AND BY WHAT MEANS" OF EACH ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION AS WELL AS NAMES OF THE PERSON/S WHO ALLEGEDLY MADE THE MISREPRESENTATION, THEIR AUTHORITY TO SPEAK, TO WHOM THEY SPOKE, WHAT THEY SAID OR WROTE, AND WHEN IT WAS SAID OR WRITTEN. THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT STATED A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS UNDER EITHER A BYSTANDER OR DIRECT VICTIM THEORY. PLAINTIFF TO FILE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT BY JULY 27, 2012. MS. SHEFFIELD TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER BY EMAIL. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: JOE VICKSTEIN CSR # 4780

Defendant Jpmorgan Chase Bank, Na'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Emily H. Sheffield, Esq., of Morrison & Foerster, Tel. 650.813.5737, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Darryl Wienke, Tel. 707.295.2022, non-party, husband to Shelley Wienke.

The matter was discussed and argued. The court modified its tentative ruling. Defendant JPmorgan Chase Bank, NA'S Demurrer To Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint is
Sustained with leave to amend as to the first through fifth causes of action, sustained WITHOUT leave to amend as to the sixth AND seventh causes of action, and overruled as to the eighth cause of action. Regarding the first cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead that Defendant unequivocally failed to contact her prior to recording the Notice of Default. Regarding the second cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead the legal effect of the contract and what portion of the contract Defendant breached, and that Plaintiff performed under the contract or was excused from performing. Regarding the third cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead a breach of the implied covenant arising out of a contract. Regarding the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead facts showing detrimental reliance. Regarding the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead fraud with particularity, including the "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means" of each alleged misrepresentation as well as names of the person/s who allegedly made the misrepresentation, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written. The sixth cause of action fails because Plaintiff has not stated a claim for negligent infliction of emotional distress under either a bystander or direct victim theory. Plaintiff to file the Amended Complaint by July 27, 2012. Ms. Sheffield to submit a proposed order by email. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Joe Vickstein CSR # 4780
